course_description:
How can businesses balance shareholder pressure for profit with stakeholder pressure for sustainable action?

This is one of the defining challenges of the twenty-first century.

In this course, students will develop the skills to evaluate the risks of inaction, identify opportunities for sustainable innovation, and embed responsible practices into strategy and operations to transition organisations towards sustainability.

This course is an introductory course for the Master of Business, Social Impact and Sustainability Field of Study. Students from other programs taking this course as an elective are welcome to attend. The course examines the role of business in society. Over the course of our industrial development, business activities have increasingly impacted the environment, driving adverse global and local environmental changes. While leading to wasteful and destructive practices, the impacts from environmental changes on businesses are now also becoming visible. Achieving corporate sustainability has therefore become a key challenge but also an opportunity for corporations. Beyond financial value, business can create social and environmental value. For example, business can create positive environmental impacts while also remaining financially sustainable.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

Based on feedback from students, we have included further exam preparation during the semester so that the students are equipped to sit an exam.

Sustainable Development Goals - UQ Business School is a proud supporter and Advanced Signatory of the United Nations Principles for Responsible Management Education (UN PRME) . As part of the largest global collaboration between business schools and the UN, the school emphasises its role in empowering students to drive societal transformation through the Sustainable Development Goals . The SDGs highlight that a thriving economy relies on a healthy environment, aiming to balance economic growth, social well-being, and environmental protection for a sustainable future.

course_aims:
This course aims to equip students with critical insights into the strategic, operational, and leadership dimensions of corporate sustainability.

Students will develop their employability capabilities in developing strategies and innovations that create sustainable value and respond to complex environmental and social challenges. Students will begin aligning ethical, legal, and financial imperatives to build a compelling business case for sustainability, implement sustainability initiatives, and foster long-term change.

learning_outcomes.description: Evaluate an organisation’s need for corporate sustainability.
Analyse the relationship between corporate activity, environmental change, and social impact.
Construct the moral, legal, and business case for sustainability using relevant ethical and regulatory frameworks.
Design change strategies that create sustainable value and support long-term organisational transformation.
In teams, co-create sustainability outcomes inclusive of diverse communities, including Australian Aboriginal and Torres Strait Island Peoples.
Reflect on the importance of leadership and collaboration in achieving sustainable action.

assessment_summary.title: Team Sustainability Role Play and Report

assessment_details.title: Reflection - Creating Change for Sustainability
assessment_details.task_description: You will need to actively manage an area of your own sustainability. Over a ten (10) day period you will need to record your experience (photos/notes). A short reflective essay will be submitted addressing key questions (provided below) including applying academic literature including the article provided in Blackboard to evaluate your experience. ADDITIONAL NOTES: This project is to be completed INDIVIDUALLY. Word length: 1500 words maximum, excluding references and appendices. This assignment needs to be academically referenced with a minimum of 3 academic references. AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

assessment_details.title: Reflection on leadership - Simulation
assessment_details.task_description: This assignment requires you to write a critical reflection on leadership for sustainability drawing on your experience in the Change Management Simulation: Power and Influence. Using your decisions, outcomes, and interactions in the simulation as empirical material, reflect on how leadership operates under conditions of organizational politics, competing interests, and unequal power. Rather than describing what happened in the simulation, your task is to analyse what the simulation reveals about leadership in promoting sustainability, particularly in relation to influence, resistance, coalition-building, and ethical judgment. ADDITIONAL NOTES: This project is to be completed INDIVIDUALLY. Word length: 1000 words maximum, excluding references and appendices. This assignment needs to be academically referenced with a minimum of 3 academic references. AI Statement Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description

learning_activities.topic: Introduction to Corporate Sustainability An overview of the course, its content and assessments.
The Drivers for Sustainability The seminar focuses on the drivers of organisational change for sustainability. Includes globalisation, regulation, SDGs, new business models and others.
The Phases of Sustainability Understand the phases of organisational sustainability. Identify key aspects used to evaluate companies and their position on the path to sustainability.

Leadership and Change Approaches to Sustainability This seminar considers change with a focus on both incremental and transformational change. We consider leadership and the appropriate types of change for a given circumstance, steps of implementation and considerations.

The Value of Sustainability Understanding the value of sustainability projects to an organisation. Consideration of how to calculate value consistent with a focus on environmental, social and financial outcomes.

Change Management This seminar explores how organizational change is shaped by power, influence, and informal networks rather than formal plans alone. We will examine why successful change depends on coalition-building and timing. This is explored through a Harvard Simulation.
IN-SEMESTER BREAK No class this week.
The Ethical and Legal Case For Action This seminar covers the ethical and legal case for action consistent with the growing trend towards a corporate focus on ESG.
Trade offs and Tensions in Sustainability Trade-offs and tensions in sustainability will be reviewed at multiple levels, individual, organisational, societal. Trade-offs in SDGs considered.
Engaging with Communities In this seminar, we explore community engagement as a relational process grounded in co-design and deep listening, where solutions emerge collaboratively.
Critical Perspectives on Corporate Sustainability Activity: Exam preparation around critically discussing business case for sustainability.

The Case for Alternative Organisations and Economy In this seminar, we examine how alternative forms of organization emerge through challenges to fossil fuel hegemony, foregrounding decarbonization, degrowth, and democratic contestation rather than market-led solutions.
Team presentations This week we will watch our team presentations.
